attrs.alt: B2B Direct Connect vs B2B Collaboration comparison

content.text: B2B Direct Connect is a trust relationship between two Microsoft Entra tenants that allows users from either tenant to access select resources in the other tenant — currently limited to Microsoft Teams Shared Channels — without creating guest user objects. The external user authenticates entirely at their home tenant and accesses the shared channel using that identity token.

B2B Direct Connect is NOT a replacement for B2B Collaboration. It is narrower in scope (Teams Shared Channels only) but avoids guest object accumulation and invitation friction for trusted partner organizations.
Key Differences: Direct Connect vs Collaboration
No — user remains purely in home tenant
Applied in resource tenant using guest identity
Sign-ins logged but no guest object events
Entra ID P1 + Teams license on both sides
Both tenants must have Microsoft Entra ID P1 or P2.
Both tenants must configure Cross-Tenant Access Settings for B2B Direct Connect — inbound AND outbound on each side.
Teams must be enabled in both tenants.
Shared Channels must be enabled in Teams admin center on both sides.
The Teams admin must create the shared channel and add external members.

Configure B2B Direct Connect via Cross-Tenant Access Settings. Both tenants must independently enable inbound and outbound direct connect. If either side is missing, shared channel access fails silently or with a generic Teams error.

Once both tenants have configured direct connect, create the shared channel in Teams and add external members. The external users are identified by their home tenant email — Teams routes the authentication through their home Entra ID tenant.

# Create a Teams Shared Channel and add an external user
# Prerequisite: B2B Direct Connect configured on both tenants

# Install Microsoft Teams PowerShell module
# Install-Module MicrosoftTeams -Force
Connect-MicrosoftTeams

# Create a shared channel in an existing team
$teamId = "ddddeeee-1111-2222-3333-444455556666"
New-TeamChannel \
    -GroupId $teamId \
    -DisplayName 'Partner Collaboration' \
    -MembershipType Shared

# Get the channel ID
$channel = Get-TeamChannel -GroupId $teamId | Where-Object { $_.DisplayName -eq "Partner Collaboration" }
$channelId = $channel.Id

# Add an external user to the shared channel
# The external user authenticates at THEIR tenant — no guest object is created
Add-TeamChannelUser \
    -GroupId $teamId \
    -ChannelId $channelId \
    -User 'partner@fabrikam.com' \
    -Role Member
Write-Host 'External user added to shared channel without creating a guest object'
After configuring your side, verify the policy is set correctly and confirm the partner has configured theirs. Shared channels fail when either side is missing the direct connect setting.

# Verify B2B Direct Connect is correctly configured on both sides
$partnerTenantId = "aaaabbbb-0000-1111-2222-333344445555"

$policy = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET \
    -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/policies/crossTenantAccessPolicy/partners/$partnerTenantId"

$dcIn  = $policy.b2bDirectConnectInbound.usersAndGroups.accessType
$dcOut = $policy.b2bDirectConnectOutbound.usersAndGroups.accessType

Write-Host "Direct Connect Inbound:  $dcIn"
Write-Host "Direct Connect Outbound: $dcOut"

if ($dcIn -eq 'allowed' -and $dcOut -eq 'allowed') {
    Write-Host 'Your side of B2B Direct Connect is correctly configured.'
    Write-Host 'Verify the partner tenant has also configured both directions.'
} else {
    Write-Warning 'B2B Direct Connect is not fully configured. Shared channels will not work.'
}
Direct Connect sign-ins appear in Sign-in Logs with CrossTenantAccessType set to 'b2bDirectConnect'. Use KQL to track activity, identify failure patterns, and measure cross-tenant channel usage.

// Monitor B2B Direct Connect sign-ins (Teams shared channels)
// Direct Connect sign-ins have a specific CrossTenantAccessType value
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where CrossTenantAccessType == 'b2bDirectConnect'
| summarize
    SignInCount     = count(),
    UniqueUsers     = dcount(UserPrincipalName),
    HomeTenantsUsed = dcount(HomeTenantId)
    by AppDisplayName, bin(TimeGenerated, 1d)
| order by TimeGenerated desc

// Find direct connect sign-ins that failed
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where CrossTenantAccessType == 'b2bDirectConnect'
| where ResultType != 0
| project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, HomeTenantId, ResultType, ResultDescription
| order by TimeGenerated desc
Apply Conditional Access using the b2bDirectConnectUser external user type to enforce MFA or other controls on direct connect sign-ins. This is separate from policies targeting B2B guest users.
External users see channel content without audit trail in resource tenant
Use sensitivity labels on channel; restrict files to DLP policies
Cannot apply group-based policies to external DC users
Use CTAS outbound restrictions scoped to specific groups
Too many shared channels become unmanageable
Require IT approval before creating shared channels
Direct connect removal does not auto-remove channel members
Remove external members from channels before revoking CTAS
Without inbound trust, external users re-auth at resource tenant
Configure isMfaAccepted if partner has equivalent MFA strength

List and remove external members from Teams Shared Channels using the MicrosoftTeams PowerShell module. Direct Connect users do not appear in Entra ID user lists — membership is only visible through Teams cmdlets.

When a business relationship ends, remove all external members from shared channels BEFORE revoking the CTAS policy. If you revoke the policy first, users are blocked immediately but channel memberships remain stale, and adding them back is required to remove them cleanly.

Apply Microsoft Purview sensitivity labels to teams that contain shared channels. Labels can restrict copying, screen capture, and external sharing within the channel. Labels set on the team apply to all its channels, including shared ones.

# Apply a sensitivity label to a Shared Channel to restrict data sharing
# Labels must be created in Microsoft Purview compliance portal first

# Get available sensitivity labels
Get-Label | Where-Object { $_.ContentType -like '*Team*' } |
    Select-Object Name, Guid | Format-Table

# Apply a label to the team (this cascades to all channels including shared)
$labelId = "ffffffff-aaaa-bbbb-cccc-111122223333"   # GUID from Get-Label
Set-TeamVirtualAppointment -Identity $teamId  # placeholder — use the admin center
# In practice: Teams Admin Center → Teams → Edit team → Sensitivity label

# Alternatively set via Graph API
$labelBody = @{
    assignedLabels = @(
        @{ labelId = $labelId }
    )
} | ConvertTo-Json

Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method PATCH \
    -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/groups/$teamId" \
    -Body $labelBody -ContentType 'application/json'
Write-Host "Sensitivity label applied to team: $teamId"

Most direct connect failures fall into three categories: CTAS policy blocking access (53003), resource tenant not configured (53011), or home tenant not configured (530021). Use this KQL query to diagnose which side has a gap.

// Troubleshoot B2B Direct Connect failures
// Error 53003 = access blocked by CTAS policy
// Error 53011 = direct connect not configured on resource tenant
// Error 530021 = direct connect not configured on home tenant
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where CrossTenantAccessType == 'b2bDirectConnect' or ResultType in ('53003','53011','530021')
| extend ErrorCategory = case(
    ResultType == '53003', 'Blocked by CTAS policy',
    ResultType == '53011', 'Direct Connect not configured in resource tenant',
    ResultType == '530021','Direct Connect not configured in home tenant',
    ResultType == 0,       'Success',
    strcat('Other error: ', ResultType))
| summarize Count = count() by ErrorCategory, HomeTenantId, AppDisplayName
| order by Count desc

As of mid-2025, B2B Direct Connect is limited to Teams Shared Channels. Microsoft has indicated plans to expand the capability to other resources, but timelines are subject to change. For other resource types, B2B Collaboration remains the mechanism.
Azure portal access via direct connect

Multi-Tenant Organizations (MTO) is a related but distinct feature that enables seamless collaboration within a single company operating multiple Entra tenants — it is not the same as B2B Direct Connect between separate organizations.
B2B Direct Connect enables Teams Shared Channels between tenants without creating guest user objects.
Both tenants must independently configure inbound and outbound direct connect in CTAS.
External users authenticate entirely at their home tenant — no invitation or redemption is needed.
Sign-ins are captured in logs with CrossTenantAccessType='b2bDirectConnect'; use the b2bDirectConnectUser CA type to enforce policy.
Direct Connect is narrower than B2B Collaboration — it does not extend to SharePoint, apps, or RBAC, only Teams Shared Channels.
Offboarding requires removing channel members separately before revoking the CTAS direct connect policy.
